steps.title: Find the slope from two points in the table
Write the line in point-slope form, then clear the denominator
steps.content:
Using point $(s, 13)$ with slope $-\frac{5}{s}$:
$$y - 13 = -\frac{5}{s}(x - s)$$
$$y - 13 = -\frac{5}{s}x + 5$$
$$y = -\frac{5}{s}x + 18$$
Multiply both sides by $s$ and rearrange:
$$sy = -5x + 18s$$
$$5x + sy = 18s$$

steps.title: Verify with the third point
steps.content:
Check $(-s, 23)$ in $5x + sy = 18s$:
$$5(-s) + s(23) = -5s + 23s = 18s \checkmark$$
All three table points satisfy the equation. That matches choice B — B is correct.
